title: LeBron outside the top 20?! Steph at 50?! How we ranked this postseason's stars

title: Sources: NBA investigating Ball trip of Adebayo
content: The NBA is investigating LaMelo Ball's trip of Bam Adebayo that resulted in the Miami Heat star's lower back injury during Tuesday's play-in game against the Charlotte Hornets, sources told ESPN's Shams Charania.

title: Bayern oust Madrid in UCL classic; PSG up next
content: Bayern Munich scored two late goals to knock record 15-time European champions Real Madrid out of the Champions League following a thrilling 4-3 win at the Allianz Arena on Wednesday.

title: Sources: Wilson staying with Aces on $5M deal
content: A'ja Wilson, the only four-time MVP in WNBA history, is staying with the Las Vegas Aces on a 3-year, $5 million supermax contract, sources told ESPN.

title: Arch '100%,' says wins came when he loosened up
content: Texas quarterback Arch Manning said Wednesday he feels "100% right now" following offseason foot surgery. He also reflected on last year, saying, "The first half of the season, I was pissed. I wasn't playing well and it wasn't fun for me."

title: Investigation underway after fan-Duran incident
content: The Twins and Major League Baseball are investigating Jarren Duran's allegation that a fan he directed his middle finger at during a game had told the Red Sox outfielder to kill himself.

title: Florida's Condon to return for senior season
content: Florida forward Alex Condon, the No. 30 prospect in ESPN's Top 100, will return for his senior year, he told ESPN on Wednesday.

title: Dolphins GM: RB Achane 'not available' for trade
content: Dolphins running back De'Von Achane is "not available" for a trade, general manager Jon-Eric Sullivan said Wednesday, as the team looks to extend its homegrown star.

title: Tigers lock up rookie McGonigle for 8 years, $150M
content: Tigers rookie Kevin McGonigle became the latest young player to get a big-money deal, agreeing Wednesday to a $150 million, eight-year contract that starts in 2027.

title: VCK pulling out all stops to keep PMK at bay in Kattumannarkoil contest
content: While the VCK is seeking to consolidate its dominance, the PMK is looking to rewire the caste equations in the northern districts. The contest looks evenly balanced, with Adi Dravidar and Muslim voters aligning with the DMK alliance, and Vanniyars consolidating behind the AIADMK front

content: No State will lose current proportional strength in Parliament, says govt. functionary; while not mentioned in the draft Bills, Home Minister has assured representatives of increase, official adds

content: AICC President and Leader of Opposition in Rajya Sabha Mallikarjun Kharge said that the INDIA bloc leaders have concluded deliberations on the Delimitation Bill, and the group has “unitedly” decided to oppose the Bill. In other news, Samrat Choudhary is sworn in as Bihar’s first BJP Chief Minister, the death toll rises to 16 in the Sakti power plant explosion in Chhattisgarh, and registration begins for the Amarnath Yatra starting July 3.

title: Slight increase in unemployment rate in March
content: Overall Labour Force Participation Rate among persons aged 15 and above was 55.4% in March, lower than 55.9% in February. The rural LFPR was 58% in March compared to 58.7% in previous month.

content: The ED searched 10 locations in Haryana’s Gurugram and Jalandhar, Punjab. An agency official said Lovely Professional University, Tetr College of Business and Masters’ Union School of Business were covered, besides the premises of Mr. Mittal.

content: The issue of permanent political solution to the Gurkha issue, which essentially means that the administrative control of the Darjeeling hills should wrest with the Gurkha people, has dominated the politics of the Darjeeling hills for several decades since the 1980s

content: ‘At a flat 33% of all seats, women’s reservation is not linked to population counts or legislatures’ expansion, it can be applied to the current or any strength of the legislatures,’ the petition signed by nearly 500 activists says

content: They will seek response to a set of 33 questions for the first phase of Population Census, 2027 —  the House Listing and Housing Operations

content: Earlier, MLC Naseer Ahmed was sacked from the post of political secretary to the CM for the same reason

content: Not only will the support commanded by ‘Sushashan Babu’ Nitish Kumar by managing complex caste dynamics and delivering on governance be hard to emulate, Samrat Choudhary faces intra-party doubts in his leadership, financial mismanagement, and other administrative issues

title: Tree-planting approaches that generate higher ecological services are less preferred by farmers: Study
content: The study examines how smallholder farmers in Karnataka make decisions about planting trees on their land

title: Navy at ‘inflection point’ as maritime threats rise, says Navy chief
content: Admiral Tripathi highlights the Navy’s steady progress towards becoming a “combat-ready, credible, cohesive, and future-ready force”

title: Last-ditch bid for peace? Pakistan army chief Asim Munir lands in Iran to convey US offer
content: A critical Iran-US ceasefire deadline looms with no agreement, fueling uncertainty. Pakistan is actively mediating, dispatching a high-level delegation to Tehran with a new US message and aiming for further talks. Key disputes over Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz persist, as both nations issue mixed signals regarding the truce's future

title: Historic! India's Vaishali wins Women's Candidates, sets up World C'ship match
content: R Vaishali created history by winning the 2026 Women’s Candidates, earning the right to challenge China’s reigning champion Ju Wenjun for the Women’s World Chess Championship crown. She beat Kateryna Lagno in the final round after Divya Deshmukh held Bibisara Assaubayeva to a draw. Vaishali now faces reigning champion Ju Wenjun and is guaranteed €28,000 plus additional bonuses.

title: Elon Musk praises Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin rocket image
content: Despite ongoing FCC complaints, Jeff Bezos shared a photo of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket, prompting a rare compliment from Elon Musk. The image signals the rocket's third mission, which will feature a reused booster. Meanwhile, SpaceX is pushing back against Amazon's FCC petition by highlighting Blue Origin's own satellite plans.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 28: Ranveer Singh film marches toward Rs. 1100 crore milestone
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' is redefining box office history with every passing day. Directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sara Arjun, the espionage thriller has become the first Hindi film ever to cross Rs. 1,000 crore in India net collections. On day 28, the film is chasing the Rs. 1,100 crore domestic milestone with a live India net of Rs. 1,096.19 crore and a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,727.93 crore.

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7 & other devices get a price hike in US: Check out new prices
content: Samsung has implemented price hikes across a wide range of its mobile devices in the US, affecting smartphones and virtually its entire tablet lineup.  While some smartphone increases target high-storage configurations, all Samsung tablets have seen price jumps, even entry-level models. This strategic shift likely reflects changing supply chain costs and a focus on higher-margin products.

title: Travis Kelce’s ring for Taylor Swift sparks rare antique diamond jewelry trend
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have reignited interest in antique diamonds, shifting focus from flawless, mass-produced stones to rare, character-rich pieces. Their engagement spotlighted vintage craftsmanship, sustainability, and uniqueness. Jewellers report rising demand, though high-quality antique diamonds remain scarce and costly. As buyers seek meaning over perfection, these historic stones are becoming prized choices, blending romance, heritage, and individuality in modern engagement trends.

title: NHL Playoffs: Lightning vs Canadiens opener pushed due to arena scheduling
content: Playoff opener between the Montreal Canadiens and Tampa bay lightening has been postponed because of issues with arena availability and the broadcast schedule. The shift moves Game 1 to Sunday to give both teams more time to prepare. Having rested key players and having all logistics done, the next question is how the re-worked schedule would affect the early momentum in this much-hyped NHL game.

title: Connor McDavid $20 million contract projection: Impact on Maple Leafs roster strategy
content: The most recent NHL news line underscores the sense that Connor McDavid would earn $20 million in the projected contract and how this has increasingly become an issue in Toronto Maple Leafs planning. Toronto seems to be getting its long-term salary cap and roster plan on track, as the Edmonton Oilers superstar keeps controlling. The case is indicative of larger NHL trade theories and new practices in team-building based on generational talent acquisition.

title: Indion-origin Arjun Singh wins Mark Bingham Award for excellence in achievement
content: Indian-origin entrepreneur Arjun Singh has earned the prestigious Mark Bingham Award for his groundbreaking work in education technology. Singh co-founded Gradescope, an AI-powered platform that revolutionizes how educators grade assignments, providing faster, more consistent feedback. Now used globally by millions, Gradescope significantly enhances digital learning and assessment processes.

title: IPL 2026: Kohli powers RCB to top as Hazlewood, Bhuvneshwar wreck LSG batters
content: Veteran bowlers Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood, alongside Rasikh Dar, spearheaded Royal Challengers Bengaluru's dominant bowling performance, bundling Lucknow Super Giants for 146 in Bengaluru. Despite a brief wobble, RCB secured a five-wicket victory, extending their unbeaten home run. Virat Kohli's quickfire 49 laid the foundation for the successful chase.

title: Love in the time of blockades: Trump seeks a ‘fat hug’ from Xi amidst Hormuz howler
content: President Trump declared the Strait of Hormuz permanently open for China and the world, claiming Beijing assured him it would cease arms shipments to Iran. This announcement follows a naval blockade, causing global economic disruption.  Trump's unpredictable approach, blending military pressure with personal diplomacy, fuels speculation about escalation or de-escalation, while European allies consider greater defense autonomy.

title: Donald Trump’s UFC Miami moment with Paulo Costa goes viral over looks
content: Donald Trump attended a UFC event in Miami and shared a viral moment with Brazilian fighter Paulo Costa after his knockout win. Trump praised Costa’s looks, calling him “too good looking to be a fighter.” While the exchange was light, his appearance drew mixed reactions online, especially as political tensions and global discussions continued at the same time.

title: 'Salute to Akhtar': Munaf hails Pak pacer for sustaining 150kph
content: Speaking about how injuries affected his career and pace, Munaf said the system in place during his early years did not provide the required support.

title: SRK’s ex-bodyguard dismisses Rs 2–2.5 crore salary claims: ‘Who pays Rs 8–10 lakh a month?’
content: Amid ongoing chatter about celebrity bodyguards allegedly earning crores every year, Yaseen Khan has weighed in, calling such claims far from reality. The ex-security aide of Shah Rukh Khan opened up about the actual payment structure in the industry, while also recalling how compensation practices have evolved over time. Reacting to social media buzz pegging bodyguards’ earnings at Rs 2–2.5 crore annually, Yaseen said such numbers are largely exaggerated and don’t reflect how the system functions.

title: How much money did R Vaishali win after Women's Candidates victory?
content: R Vaishali won the 2026 Women’s Candidates with 8.5/14, earning €28,000 plus €2,200 per half-point, taking her total beyond €40,000. Her victory also secures a World Championship clash against Ju Wenjun, offering bigger prize money and global recognition in the next stage.

title: 'Left lunatics might not like this': Trump shares AI image of Jesus hugging him
content: Former US President Donald Trump shared a new AI-generated image showing Jesus embracing him. This follows criticism for a previous image where he was depicted as Jesus healing a man. Trump defended the earlier post, claiming it was meant to represent him as a doctor. The controversial images have drawn varied reactions from different groups.

title: xQc questions Pokimane over victims' claim in Sykkuno allegations debate
content: Felix “xQc” Lengyel and Imane “Pokimane” Anys clashed online over her “victims” comment while discussing Thomas “Sykkuno” allegations. Pokimane pointed to possible multiple affected people, while xQc demanded clear proof and precise wording. Their debate, shared across Twitch and X, turned into a larger discussion about language, meaning, and how serious claims should be communicated carefully.

title: Braden 'Clavicular' situation takes worrying turn after livestream clip spreads
content: Kick streamer Braden Clavicular was hospitalized on April 14, 2026, after a suspected overdose during a livestream. Viral clips showed him unresponsive at a club. TMZ confirmed the hospitalization, while Taylor Lorenz reported he had a seizure and remains in stable condition but not fully conscious. He is under observation, and no official statement has been released yet.

title: Autistic girl finished primary school at 5, earned master’s at 11, now aims for NASA
content: A young Mexican girl, Adhara Perez Sanchez, diagnosed with autism, is captivating the world with her rapid academic achievements. She completed primary school at five and earned a master's in engineering by eleven, boasting an IQ of 162. Driven by a passion for space, Adhara dreams of working at NASA and contributing to Mars colonization efforts.

title: New 'Choudhary' of Magadh: Samarat's 'muretha' vow comes full circle with Nitish's exit
content: Samrat  Choudhary’s journey to the top has hardly followed a conventional BJP path. Unlike many leaders nurtured within the Sangh ecosystem, he comes from outside that fold. His father, veteran politician Shakuni Choudhary, was a founding member of the Samata Party and a contemporary of both Nitish Kumar and Lalu Prasad Yadav. Samrat himself began in the Rashtriya Janata Dal in the 1990s and entered government early, serving as a minister under Rabri Devi.

title: Oil price shock loading: How India's strong economic fundamentals will cushion the blow
content: S&P Global in its latest report titled India's Strong Fundamentals Would Cushion The Blow Of An Oil Shock says India is equipped to handle some strain. The report assumes a base case scenario of oil prices at an average of $130 per barrel for 2026 and $100 in 2027.

title: After three dots, Rishabh Pant walks off — what happened?
content: Skipper Rishabh Pant was forced to leave the field after a painful elbow blow during Lucknow Super Giants’ match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Wednesday. Struggling against Josh Hazlewood, Pant was hit by a short ball and walked off in pain, with Nicholas Pooran replacing him, disrupting LSG’s early momentum.

title: Alopecia Areata remedies: Home remedies to reverse bald spot in women
content: Discover natural remedies for female hair loss, from coconut oil massages to onion juice and aloe vera. The article emphasizes addressing underlying causes like stress and diet, while also advising against damaging hair practices. Patience and consistency are key, with results typically appearing within weeks.

title: Mixed signals: Trump says Iran war ending 'very soon' but 10K more troops head to Middle East
content: Over 10,000 additional US troops are deploying to the Middle East this month, including personnel aboard the USS George HW Bush and the Boxer Amphibious Ready Group. This build-up coincides with the potential end of a two-week ceasefire with Iran, as planning for ground operations, including securing enriched uranium, is under way.

title: Alcaraz opens up on hate messages after Sinner takes World No. 1 spot at Monte Carlo
content: Carlos Alcaraz admitted that online hate affected him after losing the World No. 1 ranking to Jannik Sinner at Monte-Carlo. He spoke openly about self-doubt and social media pressure. Despite the setback, he bounced back with a win in Barcelona. Sam Querrey praised his mindset, saying his acceptance of losses makes him even more dangerous moving forward.

title: 'Xi will give me big, fat hug': Trump says he opened Hormuz for China ... 'and also the world'
content: President Trump claims China is pleased with his decision to permanently open the Strait of Hormuz, stating he's acting for their benefit and the world's. He asserts China has agreed to cease weapon shipments to Iran and anticipates a warm reception from President Xi during an upcoming visit, highlighting smart cooperation over conflict.

title: 'Hissa chori': Rahul accuses Centre of 'power grab' using delimitation
content: Rahul Gandhi accused the Centre of a power grab through a delimitation bill, stating the women's reservation bill was already passed in 2023. He asserted the Congress party unequivocally supports women's reservation and condemned the proposed increase in Lok Sabha seats as 'hissa chori' from marginalized communities and certain regions.

title: World Bank Chief Economist Warns Of Hunger Risk From War In Iran
content: Iran War: The blocking of the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil supply route, has sent fertilizer prices soaring since they rely on oil-based inputs.

title: Iran Bought Chinese Satellite In Orbit, Then Used It To Target US Bases: Report
content: The satellite, known as TEE-01B, was developed and launched by the Chinese firm Earth Eye Co. before being taken over by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps' Aerospace Force.

title: Can't Hollow Out Religion In Name Of Reforms: Supreme Court In Sabarimala Case
content: Chief Justice Surya Kant remarked that perhaps the most difficult task for the court is facing is how to declare that the beliefs held by millions of people are incorrect or erroneous.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Netanyahu Says US, Israel Have 'Identical Goals' On Iran
content: US-Iran War Live: The Iran war began when the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28. Iran responded with its own strikes on Israel and Gulf states that host US bases.

title: "Turn Around Or Will Use Force": US Warning To Ships Transiting Hormuz
content: Iran has rejected the US move outright, with its Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmail Baghaei saying the country is "not 'blockadable'".

content: Tensions remain particularly high over Hormuz, a critical waterway for oil and gas that's been effectively shuttered since the start of the war almost seven weeks ago.

title: Massive Fire Rips Through Lucknow Slum, Thick Smoke Seen Miles Away
content: A massive fire broke out at a slum cluster in Lucknow's Vikas Nagar area on Wednesday evening, destroying more than two dozen huts and triggering panic among residents.

title: Adani Wind Commissions NextGen 5-MW Wind Turbine In Gujarat
content: Adani Wind has commissioned a 5-megawatt (MW) 185-metre rotor diameter wind turbine prototype at Gujarat's Mundra, featuring wind blades among the largest in India and adding to the next generation of turbine platforms emerging in the country.

content: With the current two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran scheduled to expire on April 22, the new troops will join the estimated 50,000 personnel that the Pentagon has said are involved in operations countering Iran.

title: Cross-border narco-network busted in Jammu; kingpin held
content: police busted long-standing narcotics network with alleged cross-border links . notorious gangster and drug kingpin alias Lau Gujjar also wanted in multiple criminal cases . he allegedly procured heroin from cross borders through drone droppings .

title: Kargil bloc rejects Ladakh territorial council, sticks to statehood, Sixth Schedule
content: ladakh was carved out of J&K and made a Union territory on Oct 31, 2019 following reorganisation of the former state . the region comprises Muslim-majority Kargil and predominantly Buddhist Leh .

title: VHP seeks law against illegal conversions
content:
NEW DELHI: Vishwa Hindu Parishad (VHP) on Wednesday called for a stringent nationwide law to curb illegal religious conversions, alleging that such activities pose a threat to national security. VHP joint general secretary Surendra Jain expressed concern over what he described as "continuous reports" of conversion-related activities, including allegations linked to corporate workplaces. Referring to an alleged case in Nashik involving Tata Consultancy Services, Jain claimed the incident had exposed a wider pattern that required urgent attention. The VHP also commended a group of police personnel who, it said, helped uncover the alleged Nashik case, claiming their intervention prevented more youths from being affected. Jain linked the issue to what he termed a broader, organised network extending beyond specific institutions. It argued that states which have enacted stricter anti-conversion laws have seen better control over such incidents.

End of Article

title: ED raids MP who replaced Raghav Chadha as AAP dy leader in RS
content:
NEW DELHI: ED's headquarters investigation unit in Delhi on Wednesday conducted searches at ten premises in Jalandhar and Gurgaon associated with AAP's deputy leader in Rajya Sabha Ashok Mittal and Lovely Professional University, in a suspected case of Foreign Exchange Management Act violations. Mittal is chancellor of Lovely Professional University while his son, Pratham, runs associated business schools in Gurgaon. The premises searched included those of Tetr College of Business and Masters' Union College of Business in Gurgaon, part of Lovely University group. The agency has recorded statements of people associated with the Mittals in connection with suspicious foreign transactions of the educational group. AAP recently replaced Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha with Mittal as its deputy leader in the house, claiming that the former was not raising relevant issues and not backing the party's stand in Parliament.

End of Article

title: Govt counters oppn, says South will gain LS seats under new formula
content: a formula proposes to increase the share of all states proportionately and by 50% in Lok Sabha . the share will rise from 28 to 42, one more than what it would have accrued . Andhra Pradesh, which has 25 seats now, will have 37, again four more than its expected tally .

title: 'Dangerous move': Opposition slams delimitation constitutional amendment
content: opposition demands that govt implement 33% women's reservation in legislatures based on current LS strength of 543 seats . the provision says the quota will be operationalised after 2027 census data will be available by then and delimitation within the existing 543 seat could be completed .

title: Nirav lodges application with European human rights court to prevent removal to India
content: the 55-year-old jeweller has applied to the european court of human rights . he has lodged a rule 39 application to stay his removal to the ECtHR . his original appeal against extradition got thrown out on march 25 by the high court .

title: SC stays Telangana HC order granting transit pre-arrest bail to Pawan Khera
content: a bench of justices stayed the order granting him transit anticipatory bail . the bench clarified that Khera could file a plea for anticipatories bail before a jurisdictional court, which means he has to move a court in Assam for protection .

title: Pakistan and Bangladesh migrants are faking they are gay to get asylum in the UK: Report
content: undercover reporters posed as students from Pakistan and Bangladesh whose visas were expiring but had no genuine basis to claim asylum . they unearthed how immigration advisers are charging up to £7,000 to manufacture false asylum claims for them . one adviser told a reporter: "there is no check-up to find out if the person is gay"
